design_philosophy.archetype: ARCHETYPE 4: SWISS & HIGH-CONTRAST (Modified for Dark Industrial)

headings.usage: Main titles, Section headers, Alert messages

MetricCard.styles: bg-zinc-900/50 border border-zinc-800 p-6 flex flex-col justify-between relative overflow-hidden group hover:border-zinc-700 transition-colors
MetricCard.enhancements: Add corner brackets (SVG) on hover or active state

StatusBadge.styles: inline-flex items-center gap-2 px-3 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wider bg-zinc-900 border border-zinc-800

SystemLog.styles: font-mono text-xs text-green-500/80 bg-black/80 p-4 border border-zinc-800 h-48 overflow-y-auto scrollbar-hide

TemperatureGauge.description: Radial or linear gauge for temperature

visual_enhancements.borders: 1px solid #27272a (zinc-800) with occasional 2px highlight on active sections

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text is at least 4.5:1 against background. Use lighter zincs for text.
accessibility.aria_labels: All LED indicators and charts must have aria-labels describing current state.
accessibility.reduced_motion: Disable pulse animations if prefers-reduced-motion is true.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup fonts in index.html first (Barlow Condensed, JetBrains Mono).
2. Implement the 'Bento Grid' layout for the main dashboard.
3. Use 'recharts' for the temperature history chart. Customize the tooltip to match the dark theme.
4. Create a 'useArduino' hook to handle WebSocket connection and state management.
5. Ensure the 'Emergency' mode triggers a visual alarm state (e.g., red borders, pulsing background).
6. Use Tailwind's 'animate-pulse' for the LED indicators when they are blinking.
7. Do NOT use standard Shadcn cards. Use custom 'div' containers with specific border styles to create the 'Grid Border' look.
8. Implement a 'System Log' that auto-scrolls to the newest entry.
9. Add a 'Connection Status' indicator in the header (Connected/Disconnected/Error).
10. Ensure all data updates in real-time without page refresh.
11. Strictly follow the color palette. Do not introduce random colors.
12. Use 'sonner' for toast notifications (e.g., 'Connection Established', 'Emergency Mode Activated').
universal_guidelines: Avoid generic 'dashboard' looks. Aim for 'Sci-Fi HUD' or 'Industrial Control Panel'.
Use uppercase for labels and headers to enhance the technical feel.
Group related metrics (e.g., Temperature & Fan Status) logically.
Ensure the dashboard is responsive (mobile-friendly stack, desktop grid).
